channel.title: Server on mo11om
channel.description: Recent content in Server on mo11om

item.title: RTX 5090 Ubuntu Server
item.author: user@example.com (Hugo Author)
item.description: The Problem Standing up a GPU compute server sounds straightforward until the RTX 5090 ships with kernel modules that aren’t yet merged upstream. You boot Ubuntu Server, run nvidia-smi, and get nothing — no device, no driver version, just silence. Meanwhile the storage layer has its own complexity: you want OS resilience on a fast ext4 RAID 10, but service data benefits from transparent compression that ext4 can’t offer. Two filesystems, two RAID arrays, one installation pass, and a GPU that may not cooperate until you know exactly where to look in dmesg.
